reasoning: Asteroid 2024 YR4's Earth impact probability reached a high of about 3.1% to 3.2% in February 2025. Then, new observations significantly reduced the uncertainty and the cumulative impact probability plummeted to virtually zero (around 0.005%). Because new observations inevitably shrink the error ellipse and the nominal trajectory now clearly avoids Earth, the probability is effectively zero that it will spike back up to 5% by June 30, 2025. The market last priced this at 2%, which is the minimum allowable probability bounds.
